words.example: His bibliomania filled every wall of the house.

words.definition: An obsessive desire to set fire to things.
words.example: Pyromania is a rare but serious impulse-control disorder.

words.definition: A recurrent, compulsive urge to steal.
words.example: Kleptomania is distinct from theft for profit.

words.definition: An obsession with power and grandiosity.

words.example: Dipsomania is now largely subsumed under alcoholism.

words.definition: An excessive sexual desire in women.
words.example: The term nymphomania is now clinically outdated.

words.definition: An excessive sexual desire in men.
words.example: Satyriasis pairs with the older term nymphomania.

words.definition: Exaggerated enthusiasm for or preoccupation with one thing.
words.example: His monomania about chess consumed every free hour.

words.definition: An obsession with foreign customs or people.
words.example: His xenomania led him to move abroad annually.

words.definition: An excessive admiration of the English.
An excessive fondness for French things.
words.example: Francomania set the tone in the Russian court.
Her melomania kept her at every recital.

words.definition: An abnormal sexual desire for women.
words.example: Gynomania is a term now largely superseded.

words.definition: An abnormal sexual desire for men.
words.example: Andromania mirrors gynomania as a paired term.

words.definition: An obsession with washing or bathing.
words.example: Ablutomania overlaps with OCD contamination fears.
Ergomania is popularly known as workaholism.

words.definition: A belief that one is God or divinely chosen.
An abnormal fondness for dead bodies.

words.example: Zoomania led her to adopt a dozen strays.

words.definition: A compulsive urge to pull out one's hair.
words.example: Trichotillomania is classified among body-focused repetitive behaviours.

words.definition: An obsession with certain words or names.
words.example: Poets sometimes cultivate a kind of onomatomania.
Bibliokleptomania is a bibliophile's dark twin.
Graphomania fills notebooks by the shelf-full.
Chrematomania distorted every decision he made.

words.definition: A compulsive tendency to lie or exaggerate.

words.example: Nostomania overtook her in the third year abroad.
Dromomania keeps him permanently on the move.

words.definition: An abnormal craving for poisons or drugs.
words.example: Toxicomania is another older name for addiction.

words.definition: A depressed craving for cold or winter.
words.example: Cheimomania is little used but occasionally cited.
His cynomania was clear from three rescue mastiffs.
Ailuromania was rife on the internet in the 2010s.

words.definition: Delight in words; an obsession with language.
words.example: Onomatolagnia is a bookish sort of joy.

words.definition: A morbid fascination with ugly or repulsive sights.
A compulsion to grind one's teeth.

words.example: Doramania was fashionable in nineteenth-century Vienna.
Hydromania is not a formal diagnosis but appears in older texts.

words.definition: A habit of picking at growths on the body.
An obsession with imagined bodily defects.
words.example: Dysmorphomania is now known as body dysmorphic disorder.

words.definition: An obsessive desire to hold public office.

words.example: Etheromania featured in nineteenth-century medical cases.
Gamomania led him to propose to strangers.

words.definition: An extreme form of mania with severe agitation.
A mild form of mania with elevated mood and energy.
words.example: Hypomania can be productive but risks escalation.
Logomania made every meeting run long.

words.definition: A delusion of one's own great wisdom.
words.example: Sophomania blinded him to sound advice.

words.definition: An obsessive desire to undergo surgery.
words.example: Tomomania describes patients seeking operations without need.
Her ablutomania emptied the tank daily.
Acromania is an old psychiatric term.
Agromania drove him to hike alone.
Chionomania drew him north each winter.
Dinomania is a synonym for choreomania.

words.definition: Delusion of being loved by another.
words.example: Erotomania fixated her on the actor.
Hodomania sent him around the world.
Islomania drew him to remote shores.
Melomania filled the house with records.
